ticker.modify.name: ticker.modify()
ticker.modify.syntax: ticker.modify(tickerid, session, adjustment, backadjustment, settlement_as_close) → simple string
ticker.modify.description: Creates a ticker identifier for requesting additional data for the script.

parameters.name: tickerid
parameters.description: Symbol name with exchange prefix, e.g. 'BATS:MSFT', 'NASDAQ:MSFT' or tickerid with session and adjustment from the ticker.new() function.

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: Session type. Optional argument. Possible values: session.regular, session.extended. Session type of the current chart is syminfo.session. If session is not given, then syminfo.session value is used.

parameters.name: adjustment
parameters.description: Adjustment type. Optional argument. Possible values: adjustment.none, adjustment.splits, adjustment.dividends. If adjustment is not given, then default adjustment value is used (can be different depending on particular instrument).

parameters.name: backadjustment
parameters.description: Specifies whether past contract data on continuous futures symbols is back-adjusted. This setting only affects the data from symbols with this option available on their charts. Optional. The default is backadjustment.inherit, meaning that the modified ticker ID inherits the setting from the ticker ID passed to the tickerid parameter, or it inherits the symbol's default if the tickerid does not specify this setting. Possible values: backadjustment.inherit, backadjustment.on, backadjustment.off.

parameters.name: settlement_as_close
parameters.description: Specifies whether a futures symbol's close value represents the actual closing price or the settlement price on "1D" and higher timeframes. This setting only affects the data from symbols with this option available on their charts. Optional. The default is settlement_as_close.inherit, meaning that the modified ticker ID inherits the setting from the tickerid passed into the function, or it inherits the chart symbol's default if the tickerid does not specify this setting. Possible values: settlement_as_close.inherit, settlement_as_close.on, settlement_as_close.off.

examples:
//@version=6
indicator("ticker_modify", overlay=true)
t1 = ticker.new(syminfo.prefix, syminfo.ticker, session.regular, adjustment.splits)
c1 = request.security(t1, "D", close)
t2 = ticker.modify(t1, session.extended)
c2 = request.security(t2, "2D", close)
plot(c1)
plot(c2)
overloads: ticker.modify(tickerid, session, adjustment, backadjustment, settlement_as_close) → simple string
ticker.modify(tickerid, session, adjustment, backadjustment, settlement_as_close) → series string

ticker.new.name: ticker.new()
ticker.new.syntax: ticker.new(prefix, ticker, session, adjustment, backadjustment, settlement_as_close) → simple string
ticker.new.description: Creates a ticker identifier for requesting additional data for the script.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: Exchange prefix. For example: 'BATS', 'NYSE', 'NASDAQ'. Exchange prefix of main series is syminfo.prefix.

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: Ticker name. For example 'AAPL', 'MSFT', 'EURUSD'. Ticker name of the main series is syminfo.ticker.

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: Session type. Optional argument. Possible values: session.regular, session.extended. Session type of the current chart is syminfo.session. If session is not given, then syminfo.session value is used.

parameters.name: adjustment
parameters.description: Adjustment type. Optional argument. Possible values: adjustment.none, adjustment.splits, adjustment.dividends. If adjustment is not given, then default adjustment value is used (can be different depending on particular instrument).

parameters.name: backadjustment
parameters.description: Specifies whether past contract data on continuous futures symbols is back-adjusted. This setting only affects the data from symbols with this option available on their charts. Optional. The default is backadjustment.inherit, meaning that the new ticker ID inherits the symbol's default setting. Possible values: backadjustment.inherit, backadjustment.on, backadjustment.off.

parameters.name: settlement_as_close
parameters.description: Specifies whether a futures symbol's close value represents the actual closing price or the settlement price on "1D" and higher timeframes. This setting only affects the data from symbols with this option available on their charts. Optional. The default is settlement_as_close.inherit, meaning that the new ticker ID inherits the chart symbol's default setting. Possible values: settlement_as_close.inherit, settlement_as_close.on, settlement_as_close.off.

examples:
//@version=6
indicator("ticker.new", overlay=true)
t = ticker.new(syminfo.prefix, syminfo.ticker, session.regular, adjustment.splits)
t2 = ticker.heikinashi(t)
c = request.security(t2, timeframe.period, low, barmerge.gaps_on)
plot(c, style=plot.style_linebr)
overloads: ticker.new(prefix, ticker, session, adjustment, backadjustment, settlement_as_close) → simple string
ticker.new(prefix, ticker, session, adjustment, backadjustment, settlement_as_close) → series string

ticker.pointfigure.name: ticker.pointfigure()
ticker.pointfigure.syntax: ticker.pointfigure(symbol, source, style, param, reversal) → simple string
ticker.pointfigure.description: Creates a ticker identifier for requesting Point & Figure values.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: Symbol ticker identifier.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The source for calculating Point & Figure. Possible values are: 'hl', 'close'.

parameters.name: style
parameters.description: Specifies the ticker's box size assignment method. Possible values: "ATR" for Average True Range sizing, "Traditional" to use a fixed size, or "PercentageLTP" to use a percentage of the last trading price.

parameters.name: param
parameters.description: Represents the ticker's "ATR length" value if the style value is "ATR", "Box size" value if the style is "Traditional", or "Percentage" value if the style is "PercentageLTP".

parameters.name: reversal
parameters.description: Reversal amount.

examples:
//@version=6
indicator("ticker.pointfigure", overlay=true)
pnf_tickerid = ticker.pointfigure(syminfo.tickerid, "hl", "Traditional", 1, 3)
pnf_close = request.security(pnf_tickerid, timeframe.period, close)
plot(pnf_close)
overloads: ticker.pointfigure(symbol, source, style, param, reversal) → simple string
ticker.pointfigure(symbol, source, style, param, reversal) → series string

ticker.renko.name: ticker.renko()
ticker.renko.syntax: ticker.renko(symbol, style, param, request_wicks, source) → simple string
ticker.renko.description: Creates a ticker identifier for requesting Renko values.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: Symbol ticker identifier.

parameters.name: style
parameters.description: Specifies the ticker's box size assignment method. Possible values: "ATR" for Average True Range sizing, "Traditional" to use a fixed size, or "PercentageLTP" to use a percentage of the last trading price.

parameters.name: param
parameters.description: Represents the ticker's "ATR length" value if the style value is "ATR", "Box size" value if the style is "Traditional", or "Percentage" value if the style is "PercentageLTP".

parameters.name: request_wicks
parameters.description: Specifies if wick values are returned for Renko bricks. When true, high and low values requested from a symbol using the ticker formed by this function will include wick values when they are present. When false, high and low will always be equal to either open or close. Optional. The default is false. A detailed explanation of how Renko wicks are calculated can be found in our Help Center.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The source used to calculate bricks. Optional. Possible values: "Close", "OHLC". The default is "Close".

examples:
//@version=6
indicator("ticker.renko", overlay=true)
renko_tickerid = ticker.renko(syminfo.tickerid, "ATR", 10)
renko_close = request.security(renko_tickerid, timeframe.period, close)
plot(renko_close)

//@version=6
indicator("Renko candles", overlay=false)
renko_tickerid = ticker.renko(syminfo.tickerid, "ATR", 10)
[renko_open, renko_high, renko_low, renko_close] = request.security(renko_tickerid, timeframe.period, [open, high, low, close])
plotcandle(renko_open, renko_high, renko_low, renko_close, color = renko_close > renko_open ? color.green : color.red)
overloads: ticker.renko(symbol, style, param, request_wicks, source) → simple string
ticker.renko(symbol, style, param, request_wicks, source) → series string

ticker.standard.name: ticker.standard()
ticker.standard.description: Creates a ticker to request data from a standard chart that is unaffected by modifiers like extended session, dividend adjustment, currency conversion, and the calculations of non-standard chart types: Heikin Ashi, Renko, etc. Among other things, this makes it possible to retrieve standard chart values when the script is running on a non-standard chart.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: A ticker ID to be converted into its standard form. Optional. The default is syminfo.tickerid.

time.name: time()
time.syntax: time(timeframe, session, bars_back, timeframe_bars_back) → series int
time.description: Returns the opening UNIX timestamp for the specified timeframe and session, or na if the time point is outside the session.

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: The timeframe of the timestamp calculation. If the value is an empty string, the function uses the script's main timeframe.

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: Optional. The session string for filtering times. The function returns a timestamp if the time is in the specified session, or na if the time is outside the session. If the argument is an empty string, the function uses the default, which is the symbol's session.

parameters.name: bars_back
parameters.description: Optional. The bar offset on the script's main timeframe. If the value is positive, the function finds the bar that is N bars before the current bar on the main timeframe, then retrieves the timestamp of the corresponding bar on the timeframe specified by the timeframe argument. If the value is a negative number from -1 to -500, the function calculates the expected timestamp of the timeframe bar corresponding to N bars after the current bar on the main timeframe. The default is 0.

parameters.name: timeframe_bars_back
parameters.description: Optional. The additional bar offset on the timeframe specified by the timeframe argument. If the value is positive, the function retrieves the timestamp of the bar that is N timeframe bars before the one corresponding to the bars_back offset. If the value is a negative number from -1 to -500, the function calculates the expected timestamp of the timeframe bar that is N timeframe bars after the one corresponding to the bars_back offset. The default is 0.

examples:
//@version=6
indicator("Time", overlay=true)
t1 = time(timeframe.period, "0000-0000:23456")
bgcolor(not na(t1) ? color.new(color.blue, 90) : na)

//@version=6
indicator("Time", overlay=true)
t1 = time(timeframe.period, "1000-1100,1400-1500:23456")
bgcolor(not na(t1) ? color.new(color.blue, 90) : na)
overloads: time(timeframe, session, bars_back, timeframe_bars_back) → series int
time(timeframe, session, timezone, bars_back, timeframe_bars_back) → series int

time_close.name: time_close()
time_close.syntax: time_close(timeframe, session, bars_back, timeframe_bars_back) → series int
time_close.description: Returns the closing UNIX timestamp for the specified timeframe and session, or na if the time point is outside the session. On tick charts and price-based charts such as Renko, line break, Kagi, point & figure, and range, the function returns na on the latest realtime bar because the future closing time is unpredictable. However, it returns a valid timestamp for any previous bar.

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: The timeframe of the timestamp calculation. If the value is an empty string, the function uses the script's main timeframe.

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: Optional. The session string for filtering times. The function returns a timestamp if the time is in the specified session, or na if the time is outside the session. If the argument is an empty string, the function uses the default, which is the symbol's session.

parameters.name: bars_back
parameters.description: Optional. The bar offset on the script's main timeframe. If the value is positive, the function finds the bar that is N bars before the current bar on the main timeframe, then retrieves the timestamp of the corresponding bar on the timeframe specified by the timeframe argument. If the value is a negative number from -1 to -500, the function calculates the expected timestamp of the timeframe bar corresponding to N bars after the current bar on the main timeframe. The default is 0.

parameters.name: timeframe_bars_back
parameters.description: Optional. The additional bar offset on the timeframe specified by the timeframe argument. If the value is positive, the function retrieves the timestamp of the bar that is N timeframe bars before the one corresponding to the bars_back offset. If the value is a negative number from -1 to -500, the function calculates the expected timestamp of the timeframe bar that is N timeframe bars after the one corresponding to the bars_back offset. The default is 0.

examples:
//@version=6
indicator("Time", overlay=true)
t1 = time_close(timeframe.period, "1200-1300", "America/New_York")
bgcolor(not na(t1) ? color.new(color.blue, 90) : na)
overloads: time_close(timeframe, session, bars_back, timeframe_bars_back) → series int
time_close(timeframe, session, timezone, bars_back, timeframe_bars_back) → series int

timeframe.change.name: timeframe.change()
timeframe.change.description: Detects changes in the specified timeframe.

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: String formatted according to the User manual's timeframe string specifications.

timeframe.from_seconds.name: timeframe.from_seconds()
timeframe.from_seconds.description: Converts a number of seconds into a valid timeframe string.

parameters.name: seconds
parameters.description: The number of seconds in the timeframe.

examples:
//@version=6
indicator("HTF Close", "", true)
int chartTf = timeframe.in_seconds()
string tfTimes5 = timeframe.from_seconds(chartTf * 5)
float htfClose = request.security(syminfo.tickerid, tfTimes5, close)
plot(htfClose)

timeframe.in_seconds.name: timeframe.in_seconds()
timeframe.in_seconds.description: Converts a timeframe string into seconds.

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: Timeframe string in timeframe string specifications format. Optional. The default is timeframe.period.

timestamp.name: timestamp()
timestamp.description: Function timestamp returns UNIX time of specified date and time.

parameters.name: dateString
parameters.description: A string containing the date and, optionally, the time and time zone. Its format must comply with either the IETF RFC 2822 or ISO 8601 standards ("DD MMM YYYY hh:mm:ss ±hhmm" or "YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss±hh:mm", so "20 Feb 2020" or "2020-02-20"). If no time is supplied, "00:00" is used. If no time zone is supplied, GMT+0 will be used. Note that this diverges from the usual behavior of the function where it returns time in the exchange's timezone.

examples:
//@version=6
indicator("timestamp")
plot(timestamp(2016, 01, 19, 09, 30), linewidth=3, color=color.green)
plot(timestamp(syminfo.timezone, 2016, 01, 19, 09, 30), color=color.blue)
plot(timestamp(2016, 01, 19, 09, 30), color=color.yellow)
plot(timestamp("GMT+6", 2016, 01, 19, 09, 30))
plot(timestamp(2019, 06, 19, 09, 30, 15), color=color.lime)
plot(timestamp("GMT+3", 2019, 06, 19, 09, 30, 15), color=color.fuchsia)
plot(timestamp("Feb 01 2020 22:10:05"))
plot(timestamp("2011-10-10T14:48:00"))
plot(timestamp("04 Dec 1995 00:12:00 GMT+5"))
overloads: timestamp(year, month, day, hour, minute, second) → simple int
timestamp(year, month, day, hour, minute, second) → series int
timestamp(timezone, year, month, day, hour, minute, second) → simple int
timestamp(timezone, year, month, day, hour, minute, second) → series int

volume_row.buy_volume.name: volume_row.buy_volume()
volume_row.buy_volume.description: Calculates the total "buy" volume for the volume footprint row represented by a volume_row object.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The reference (ID) of the volume_row object to analyze.

volume_row.delta.name: volume_row.delta()
volume_row.delta.description: Calculates the volume delta for the volume footprint row represented by a volume_row object. The value represents the difference between the row's "buy" volume and "sell" volume. A positive value indicates that the "buy" volume for the row exceeds the "sell" volume, and a negative value indicates the opposite.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The reference (ID) of the volume_row object to analyze.

volume_row.down_price.name: volume_row.down_price()
volume_row.down_price.description: Retrieves the lower price level of the volume footprint row represented by a volume_row object.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The reference (ID) of the volume_row object to analyze.

volume_row.has_buy_imbalance.name: volume_row.has_buy_imbalance()
volume_row.has_buy_imbalance.description: Checks whether the volume footprint row represented by a volume_row object has a "buy" imbalance, based on the imbalance_percent argument of the request.footprint() call that the object depends on. Returns true if the row's "buy" volume exceeds the "sell" volume of the row below it in the footprint by the specified percentage, and false otherwise.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The reference (ID) of the volume_row object to analyze.

volume_row.has_sell_imbalance.name: volume_row.has_sell_imbalance()
volume_row.has_sell_imbalance.description: Checks whether the volume footprint row represented by a volume_row object has a sell imbalance, based on the imbalance_percent argument of the request.footprint() call that the object depends on. Returns true if the row's "sell" volume exceeds the "buy" volume of the row above it in the footprint by the specified percentage, and false otherwise.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The reference (ID) of the volume_row object to analyze.

volume_row.sell_volume.name: volume_row.sell_volume()
volume_row.sell_volume.description: Calculates the total "sell" volume for the volume footprint row represented by a volume_row object.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The reference (ID) of the volume_row object to analyze.

volume_row.total_volume.name: volume_row.total_volume()
volume_row.total_volume.description: Calculates the sum of the "buy" and "sell" volume for the volume footprint row represented by a volume_row object.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The reference (ID) of the volume_row object to analyze.

volume_row.up_price.name: volume_row.up_price()
volume_row.up_price.description: Retrieves the upper price level of the volume footprint row represented by a volume_row object.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The reference (ID) of the volume_row object to analyze.

weekofyear.name: weekofyear()
weekofyear.description: Calculates the week number of the year, in a specified time zone, from a UNIX timestamp.

parameters.name: time
parameters.description: A UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Optional. Specifies the time zone of the returned week number. The value can be a time zone string in UTC/GMT offset notation (e.g., "UTC-5") or IANA time zone database notation (e.g., "America/New_York"). The default is syminfo.timezone.

parameters.name: time
parameters.description: UNIX time in milliseconds.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Allows adjusting the returned value to a time zone specified in either UTC/GMT notation (e.g., "UTC-5", "GMT+0530") or as an IANA time zone database name (e.g., "America/New_York"). Optional. The default is syminfo.timezone.

ask.name: ask
ask.description: The ask price at the time of the current tick, which represents the lowest price an active seller will accept for the instrument at its current value. This information is available only on the "1T" timeframe. On other timeframes, the variable's value is na.

bar_index.name: bar_index
bar_index.description: Current bar index. Numbering is zero-based, index of the first bar is 0.

barstate.isconfirmed.name: barstate.isconfirmed
barstate.isconfirmed.description: Returns true if the script is calculating the last (closing) update of the current bar. The next script calculation will be on the new bar data.

barstate.isfirst.name: barstate.isfirst
barstate.isfirst.description: Returns true if current bar is first bar in barset, false otherwise.

barstate.ishistory.name: barstate.ishistory
barstate.ishistory.description: Returns true if current bar is a historical bar, false otherwise.

barstate.islast.name: barstate.islast
barstate.islast.description: Returns true if current bar is the last bar in barset, false otherwise. This condition is true for all real-time bars in barset.

barstate.islastconfirmedhistory.name: barstate.islastconfirmedhistory
barstate.islastconfirmedhistory.description: Returns true if script is executing on the dataset's last bar when market is closed, or script is executing on the bar immediately preceding the real-time bar, if market is open. Returns false otherwise.

barstate.isnew.name: barstate.isnew
barstate.isnew.description: Returns true if script is currently calculating on new bar, false otherwise. This variable is true when calculating on historical bars or on first update of a newly generated real-time bar.

barstate.isrealtime.name: barstate.isrealtime
barstate.isrealtime.description: Returns true if current bar is a real-time bar, false otherwise.

bid.name: bid
bid.description: The bid price at the time of the current tick, which represents the highest price an active buyer is willing to pay for the instrument at its current value. This information is available only on the "1T" timeframe. On other timeframes, the variable's value is na.

box.all.name: box.all
box.all.description: Returns an array filled with all the current boxes drawn by the script.

chart.bg_color.name: chart.bg_color
chart.bg_color.description: Returns the color of the chart's background from the "Chart settings/Appearance/Background" field. When a gradient is selected, the middle point of the gradient is returned.

chart.fg_color.name: chart.fg_color
chart.fg_color.description: Returns a color providing optimal contrast with chart.bg_color.

chart.is_heikinashi.name: chart.is_heikinashi
chart.is_heikinashi.description: Returns true if the chart type is Heikin Ashi, false otherwise.

chart.is_kagi.name: chart.is_kagi
chart.is_kagi.description: Returns true if the chart type is Kagi, false otherwise.

chart.is_linebreak.name: chart.is_linebreak
chart.is_linebreak.description: Returns true if the chart type is Line break, false otherwise.

chart.is_pnf.name: chart.is_pnf
chart.is_pnf.description: Returns true if the chart type is Point & figure, false otherwise.

chart.is_range.name: chart.is_range
chart.is_range.description: Returns true if the chart type is Range, false otherwise.

chart.is_renko.name: chart.is_renko
chart.is_renko.description: Returns true if the chart type is Renko, false otherwise.

chart.is_standard.name: chart.is_standard
chart.is_standard.description: Returns true if the chart type is not one of the following: Renko, Kagi, Line break, Point & figure, Range, Heikin Ashi; false otherwise.

chart.left_visible_bar_time.name: chart.left_visible_bar_time
chart.left_visible_bar_time.description: The time of the leftmost bar currently visible on the chart.

chart.right_visible_bar_time.name: chart.right_visible_bar_time
chart.right_visible_bar_time.description: The time of the rightmost bar currently visible on the chart.

close.name: close
close.description: Close price of the current bar when it has closed, or last traded price of a yet incomplete, realtime bar.

dayofmonth.name: dayofmonth
dayofmonth.description: The day number of the month, in the exchange time zone, calculated from the bar's opening UNIX timestamp.

dayofweek.name: dayofweek
dayofweek.description: The day number of the week, in the exchange time zone, calculated from the bar's opening UNIX timestamp.

dividends.future_amount.name: dividends.future_amount
dividends.future_amount.description: Returns the payment amount of the upcoming dividend in the currency of the current instrument, or na if this data isn't available.

dividends.future_ex_date.name: dividends.future_ex_date
dividends.future_ex_date.description: Returns the Ex-dividend date (Ex-date) of the current instrument's next dividend payment, or na if this data isn't available. Ex-dividend date signifies when investors are no longer entitled to a payout from the most recent dividend. Only those who purchased shares before this day are entitled to the dividend payment.

dividends.future_pay_date.name: dividends.future_pay_date
dividends.future_pay_date.description: Returns the Payment date (Pay date) of the current instrument's next dividend payment, or na if this data isn't available. Payment date signifies the day when eligible investors will receive the dividend payment.

earnings.future_eps.name: earnings.future_eps
earnings.future_eps.description: Returns the estimated Earnings per Share of the next earnings report in the currency of the instrument, or na if this data isn't available.

earnings.future_period_end_time.name: earnings.future_period_end_time
earnings.future_period_end_time.description: Checks the data for the next earnings report and returns the UNIX timestamp of the day when the financial period covered by those earnings ends, or na if this data isn't available.

earnings.future_revenue.name: earnings.future_revenue
earnings.future_revenue.description: Returns the estimated Revenue of the next earnings report in the currency of the instrument, or na if this data isn't available.

earnings.future_time.name: earnings.future_time
earnings.future_time.description: Returns a UNIX timestamp indicating the expected time of the next earnings report, or na if this data isn't available.

hlc3.name: hlc3
hlc3.description: Is a shortcut for (high + low + close)/3

hlcc4.name: hlcc4
hlcc4.description: Is a shortcut for (high + low + close + close)/4

hour.name: hour
hour.description: Current bar hour in exchange timezone.

label.all.name: label.all
label.all.description: Returns an array filled with all the current labels drawn by the script.

last_bar_index.name: last_bar_index
last_bar_index.description: Bar index of the last chart bar. Bar indices begin at zero on the first bar.

last_bar_time.name: last_bar_time
last_bar_time.description: Time in UNIX format of the last chart bar. It is the number of milliseconds that have elapsed since 00:00:00 UTC, 1 January 1970.

line.all.name: line.all
line.all.description: Returns an array filled with all the current lines drawn by the script.

linefill.all.name: linefill.all
linefill.all.description: Returns an array filled with all the current linefill objects drawn by the script.

minute.name: minute
minute.description: Current bar minute in exchange timezone.

month.name: month
month.description: Current bar month in exchange timezone.

na.name: na
na.description: A keyword signifying "not available", indicating that a variable has no assigned value.

ohlc4.name: ohlc4
ohlc4.description: Is a shortcut for (open + high + low + close)/4

polyline.all.name: polyline.all
polyline.all.description: Returns an array containing all current polyline instances drawn by the script.

second.name: second
second.description: Current bar second in exchange timezone.

session.isfirstbar.name: session.isfirstbar
session.isfirstbar.description: Returns true if the current bar is the first bar of the day's session, false otherwise. If extended session information is used, only returns true on the first bar of the pre-market bars.

session.isfirstbar_regular.name: session.isfirstbar_regular
session.isfirstbar_regular.description: Returns true on the first regular session bar of the day, false otherwise. The result is the same whether extended session information is used or not.

session.islastbar.name: session.islastbar
session.islastbar.description: Returns true if the current bar is the last bar of the day's session, false otherwise. If extended session information is used, only returns true on the last bar of the post-market bars.

session.islastbar_regular.name: session.islastbar_regular
session.islastbar_regular.description: Returns true on the last regular session bar of the day, false otherwise. The result is the same whether extended session information is used or not.

session.ismarket.name: session.ismarket
session.ismarket.description: Returns true if the current bar is a part of the regular trading hours (i.e. market hours), false otherwise.

session.ispostmarket.name: session.ispostmarket
session.ispostmarket.description: Returns true if the current bar is a part of the post-market, false otherwise. On non-intraday charts always returns false.

session.ispremarket.name: session.ispremarket
session.ispremarket.description: Returns true if the current bar is a part of the pre-market, false otherwise. On non-intraday charts always returns false.

strategy.account_currency.name: strategy.account_currency
strategy.account_currency.description: Returns the currency used to calculate results, which can be set in the strategy's properties.

strategy.avg_losing_trade.name: strategy.avg_losing_trade
strategy.avg_losing_trade.description: Returns the average amount of money lost per losing trade. Calculated as the sum of losses divided by the number of losing trades.

strategy.avg_losing_trade_percent.name: strategy.avg_losing_trade_percent
strategy.avg_losing_trade_percent.description: Returns the average percentage loss per losing trade. Calculated as the sum of loss percentages divided by the number of losing trades.

strategy.avg_trade.name: strategy.avg_trade
strategy.avg_trade.description: Returns the average amount of money gained or lost per trade. Calculated as the sum of all profits and losses divided by the number of closed trades.

strategy.avg_trade_percent.name: strategy.avg_trade_percent
strategy.avg_trade_percent.description: Returns the average percentage gain or loss per trade. Calculated as the sum of all profit and loss percentages divided by the number of closed trades.

strategy.avg_winning_trade.name: strategy.avg_winning_trade
strategy.avg_winning_trade.description: Returns the average amount of money gained per winning trade. Calculated as the sum of profits divided by the number of winning trades.

strategy.avg_winning_trade_percent.name: strategy.avg_winning_trade_percent
strategy.avg_winning_trade_percent.description: Returns the average percentage gain per winning trade. Calculated as the sum of profit percentages divided by the number of winning trades.

strategy.closedtrades.name: strategy.closedtrades
strategy.closedtrades.description: Number of trades, which were closed for the whole trading range.

strategy.closedtrades.first_index.name: strategy.closedtrades.first_index
strategy.closedtrades.first_index.description: The index, or trade number, of the first (oldest) trade listed in the List of Trades. This number is usually zero. If more trades than the allowed limit have been closed, the oldest trades are removed, and this number is the index of the oldest remaining trade.

strategy.eventrades.name: strategy.eventrades
strategy.eventrades.description: Number of breakeven trades for the whole trading range.

strategy.grossloss.name: strategy.grossloss
strategy.grossloss.description: Total currency value of all completed losing trades.

strategy.grossloss_percent.name: strategy.grossloss_percent
strategy.grossloss_percent.description: The total value of all completed losing trades, expressed as a percentage of the initial capital.

strategy.grossprofit.name: strategy.grossprofit
strategy.grossprofit.description: Total currency value of all completed winning trades.

strategy.grossprofit_percent.name: strategy.grossprofit_percent
strategy.grossprofit_percent.description: The total currency value of all completed winning trades, expressed as a percentage of the initial capital.

strategy.initial_capital.name: strategy.initial_capital
strategy.initial_capital.description: The amount of initial capital set in the strategy properties.

strategy.losstrades.name: strategy.losstrades
strategy.losstrades.description: Number of unprofitable trades for the whole trading range.

strategy.margin_liquidation_price.name: strategy.margin_liquidation_price
strategy.margin_liquidation_price.description: When margin is used in a strategy, returns the price point where a simulated margin call will occur and liquidate enough of the position to meet the margin requirements.

strategy.max_contracts_held_all.name: strategy.max_contracts_held_all
strategy.max_contracts_held_all.description: Maximum number of contracts/shares/lots/units in one trade for the whole trading range.

strategy.max_contracts_held_long.name: strategy.max_contracts_held_long
strategy.max_contracts_held_long.description: Maximum number of contracts/shares/lots/units in one long trade for the whole trading range.

strategy.max_contracts_held_short.name: strategy.max_contracts_held_short
strategy.max_contracts_held_short.description: Maximum number of contracts/shares/lots/units in one short trade for the whole trading range.

strategy.max_drawdown.name: strategy.max_drawdown
strategy.max_drawdown.description: Maximum equity drawdown value for the whole trading range.

strategy.max_drawdown_percent.name: strategy.max_drawdown_percent
strategy.max_drawdown_percent.description: The maximum equity drawdown value for the whole trading range, expressed as a percentage and calculated by formula: Lowest Value During Trade / (Entry Price x Quantity) * 100.

strategy.max_runup.name: strategy.max_runup
strategy.max_runup.description: Maximum equity run-up value for the whole trading range.

strategy.max_runup_percent.name: strategy.max_runup_percent
strategy.max_runup_percent.description: The maximum equity run-up value for the whole trading range, expressed as a percentage and calculated by formula: Highest Value During Trade / (Entry Price x Quantity) * 100.

strategy.netprofit.name: strategy.netprofit
strategy.netprofit.description: Total currency value of all completed trades.

strategy.netprofit_percent.name: strategy.netprofit_percent
strategy.netprofit_percent.description: The total value of all completed trades, expressed as a percentage of the initial capital.

strategy.openprofit.name: strategy.openprofit
strategy.openprofit.description: Current unrealized profit or loss for all open positions.

strategy.openprofit_percent.name: strategy.openprofit_percent
strategy.openprofit_percent.description: The current unrealized profit or loss for all open positions, expressed as a percentage and calculated by formula: openPL / realizedEquity * 100.

strategy.opentrades.name: strategy.opentrades
strategy.opentrades.description: Number of market position entries, which were not closed and remain opened. If there is no open market position, 0 is returned.

strategy.opentrades.capital_held.name: strategy.opentrades.capital_held
strategy.opentrades.capital_held.description: Returns the capital amount currently held by open trades.

strategy.position_avg_price.name: strategy.position_avg_price
strategy.position_avg_price.description: Average entry price of current market position. If the market position is flat, 'NaN' is returned.

strategy.position_entry_name.name: strategy.position_entry_name
strategy.position_entry_name.description: Name of the order that initially opened current market position.

strategy.position_size.name: strategy.position_size
strategy.position_size.description: Direction and size of the current market position. If the value is > 0, the market position is long. If the value is < 0, the market position is short. The absolute value is the number of contracts/shares/lots/units in trade (position size).

strategy.wintrades.name: strategy.wintrades
strategy.wintrades.description: Number of profitable trades for the whole trading range.

syminfo.basecurrency.name: syminfo.basecurrency
syminfo.basecurrency.description: Returns a string containing the code representing the symbol's base currency (i.e., the traded currency or coin) if the instrument is a Forex or Crypto pair or a derivative based on such a pair. Otherwise, it returns an empty string. For example, this variable returns "EUR" for "EURJPY", "BTC" for "BTCUSDT", "CAD" for "CME:6C1!", and "" for "NASDAQ:AAPL".

syminfo.country.name: syminfo.country
syminfo.country.description: Returns the two-letter code of the country where the symbol is traded, in the ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format, or na if the exchange is not directly tied to a specific country. For example, on "NASDAQ:AAPL" it will return "US", on "LSE:AAPL" it will return "GB", and on "BITSTAMP:BTCUSD it will return na.

syminfo.currency.name: syminfo.currency
syminfo.currency.description: Returns a string containing the code representing the currency of the symbol's prices. For example, this variable returns "USD" for "NASDAQ:AAPL" and "JPY" for "EURJPY".

syminfo.current_contract.name: syminfo.current_contract
syminfo.current_contract.description: The ticker identifier of the underlying contract, if the current symbol is a continuous futures contract; na otherwise.

syminfo.employees.name: syminfo.employees
syminfo.employees.description: The number of employees the company has.

syminfo.expiration_date.name: syminfo.expiration_date
syminfo.expiration_date.description: A UNIX timestamp representing the start of the last day of the current futures contract. This variable is only compatible with non-continuous futures symbols. On other symbols, it returns na.

syminfo.industry.name: syminfo.industry
syminfo.industry.description: Returns the industry of the symbol, or na if the symbol has no industry. Example: "Internet Software/Services", "Packaged software", "Integrated Oil", "Motor Vehicles", etc. These are the same values one can see in the chart's "Symbol info" window.

syminfo.isin.name: syminfo.isin
syminfo.isin.description: Holds a string representing a symbol's associated International Securities Identification Number (ISIN), or an empty string if there is no ISIN information available for the symbol. An ISIN is a 12-character alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies a security globally. Unlike ticker symbols, which can vary across exchanges, the ISIN for a security is consistent across exchanges. As such, programmers can use the ISIN to identify an underlying financial instrument, regardless of the exchange or the symbol name listed by an exchange.

syminfo.main_tickerid.name: syminfo.main_tickerid
syminfo.main_tickerid.description: A ticker identifier representing the current chart's symbol. The value contains an exchange prefix and a symbol name, separated by a colon (e.g., "NASDAQ:AAPL"). It can also include information about data modifications such as dividend adjustment, non-standard chart type, currency conversion, etc. Unlike syminfo.tickerid, this variable's value does not change when used in the expression argument of a request.*() function call.

syminfo.mincontract.name: syminfo.mincontract
syminfo.mincontract.description: The smallest amount of the current symbol that can be traded. This limit is set by the exchange. For cryptocurrencies, it is often less than 1 token. For most other types of asset, it is often 1.

syminfo.minmove.name: syminfo.minmove
syminfo.minmove.description: Returns a whole number used to calculate the smallest increment between a symbol's price movements (syminfo.mintick). It is the numerator in the syminfo.mintick formula: syminfo.minmove / syminfo.pricescale = syminfo.mintick.

syminfo.mintick.name: syminfo.mintick
syminfo.mintick.description: Min tick value for the current symbol.

syminfo.pointvalue.name: syminfo.pointvalue
syminfo.pointvalue.description: The chart price of a security multiplied by the point value equals the actual price of the traded security.

syminfo.prefix.name: syminfo.prefix
syminfo.prefix.description: Prefix of current symbol name (i.e. for 'CME_EOD:TICKER' prefix is 'CME_EOD').

syminfo.pricescale.name: syminfo.pricescale
syminfo.pricescale.description: Returns a whole number used to calculate the smallest increment between a symbol's price movements (syminfo.mintick). It is the denominator in the syminfo.mintick formula: syminfo.minmove / syminfo.pricescale = syminfo.mintick.

syminfo.recommendations_buy.name: syminfo.recommendations_buy
syminfo.recommendations_buy.description: The number of analysts who gave the current symbol a "Buy" rating.

syminfo.recommendations_buy_strong.name: syminfo.recommendations_buy_strong
syminfo.recommendations_buy_strong.description: The number of analysts who gave the current symbol a "Strong Buy" rating.

syminfo.recommendations_date.name: syminfo.recommendations_date
syminfo.recommendations_date.description: The starting date of the last set of recommendations for the current symbol.

syminfo.recommendations_hold.name: syminfo.recommendations_hold
syminfo.recommendations_hold.description: The number of analysts who gave the current symbol a "Hold" rating.

syminfo.recommendations_sell.name: syminfo.recommendations_sell
syminfo.recommendations_sell.description: The number of analysts who gave the current symbol a "Sell" rating.

syminfo.recommendations_sell_strong.name: syminfo.recommendations_sell_strong
syminfo.recommendations_sell_strong.description: The number of analysts who gave the current symbol a "Strong Sell" rating.

syminfo.recommendations_total.name: syminfo.recommendations_total
syminfo.recommendations_total.description: The total number of recommendations for the current symbol.

syminfo.root.name: syminfo.root
syminfo.root.description: Root for derivatives like futures contract. For other symbols returns the same value as syminfo.ticker.

syminfo.sector.name: syminfo.sector
syminfo.sector.description: Returns the sector of the symbol, or na if the symbol has no sector. Example: "Electronic Technology", "Technology services", "Energy Minerals", "Consumer Durables", etc. These are the same values one can see in the chart's "Symbol info" window.

syminfo.session.name: syminfo.session
syminfo.session.description: Session type of the chart main series. Possible values are session.regular, session.extended.

syminfo.shareholders.name: syminfo.shareholders
syminfo.shareholders.description: The number of shareholders the company has.

syminfo.shares_outstanding_float.name: syminfo.shares_outstanding_float
syminfo.shares_outstanding_float.description: The total number of shares outstanding a company has available, excluding any of its restricted shares.

syminfo.shares_outstanding_total.name: syminfo.shares_outstanding_total
syminfo.shares_outstanding_total.description: The total number of shares outstanding a company has available, including restricted shares held by insiders, major shareholders, and employees.

syminfo.target_price_average.name: syminfo.target_price_average
syminfo.target_price_average.description: The average of the last yearly price targets for the symbol predicted by analysts.

syminfo.target_price_date.name: syminfo.target_price_date
syminfo.target_price_date.description: The starting date of the last price target prediction for the current symbol.

syminfo.target_price_estimates.name: syminfo.target_price_estimates
syminfo.target_price_estimates.description: The latest total number of price target predictions for the current symbol.

syminfo.target_price_high.name: syminfo.target_price_high
syminfo.target_price_high.description: The last highest yearly price target for the symbol predicted by analysts.

syminfo.target_price_low.name: syminfo.target_price_low
syminfo.target_price_low.description: The last lowest yearly price target for the symbol predicted by analysts.

syminfo.target_price_median.name: syminfo.target_price_median
syminfo.target_price_median.description: The median of the last yearly price targets for the symbol predicted by analysts.

syminfo.ticker.name: syminfo.ticker
syminfo.ticker.description: Symbol name without exchange prefix, e.g. 'MSFT'.

syminfo.tickerid.name: syminfo.tickerid
syminfo.tickerid.description: A ticker identifier representing the chart's symbol or a requested symbol, depending on how the script uses it. The variable's value represents a requested dataset's ticker ID when used in the expression argument of a request.*() function call. Otherwise, it represents the chart's ticker ID. The value contains an exchange prefix and a symbol name, separated by a colon (e.g., "NASDAQ:AAPL"). It can also include information about data modifications such as dividend adjustment, non-standard chart type, currency conversion, etc.

syminfo.timezone.name: syminfo.timezone
syminfo.timezone.description: Timezone of the exchange of the chart main series. Possible values see in timestamp().

syminfo.type.name: syminfo.type
syminfo.type.description: The type of market the symbol belongs to. The values are "stock", "fund", "dr", "right", "bond", "warrant", "structured", "index", "forex", "futures", "spread", "economic", "fundamental", "crypto", "spot", "swap", "option", "commodity".

syminfo.volumetype.name: syminfo.volumetype
syminfo.volumetype.description: Volume type of the current symbol. Possible values are: "base" for base currency, "quote" for quote currency, "tick" for the number of transactions, and "n/a" when there is no volume or its type is not specified.

ta.tr.name: ta.tr
ta.tr.description: True range, equivalent to ta.tr(handle_na = false). It is calculated as math.max(high - low, math.abs(high - close[1]), math.abs(low - close[1])).

ta.vwap.name: ta.vwap
ta.vwap.description: Volume Weighted Average Price. It uses hlc3 as its source series.

table.all.name: table.all
table.all.description: Returns an array filled with all the current tables drawn by the script.

time.name: time
time.description: Current bar time in UNIX format. It is the number of milliseconds that have elapsed since 00:00:00 UTC, 1 January 1970.

time_close.name: time_close
time_close.description: The time of the current bar's close in UNIX format. It represents the number of milliseconds elapsed since 00:00:00 UTC, 1 January 1970. On tick charts and price-based charts such as Renko, line break, Kagi, point & figure, and range, this variable's series holds an na timestamp for the latest realtime bar (because the future closing time is unpredictable), but valid timestamps for all previous bars.

time_tradingday.name: time_tradingday
time_tradingday.description: The timestamp that represents 00:00 UTC of the trading day the current bar belongs to, in UNIX format (the number of milliseconds that have elapsed since 00:00:00 UTC, 1 January 1970).

timeframe.isdaily.name: timeframe.isdaily
timeframe.isdaily.description: Returns true if current resolution is a daily resolution, false otherwise.

timeframe.isdwm.name: timeframe.isdwm
timeframe.isdwm.description: Returns true if current resolution is a daily or weekly or monthly resolution, false otherwise.

timeframe.isintraday.name: timeframe.isintraday
timeframe.isintraday.description: Returns true if current resolution is an intraday (minutes or seconds) resolution, false otherwise.

timeframe.isminutes.name: timeframe.isminutes
timeframe.isminutes.description: Returns true if current resolution is a minutes resolution, false otherwise.

timeframe.ismonthly.name: timeframe.ismonthly
timeframe.ismonthly.description: Returns true if current resolution is a monthly resolution, false otherwise.

timeframe.isseconds.name: timeframe.isseconds
timeframe.isseconds.description: Returns true if current resolution is a seconds resolution, false otherwise.

timeframe.isticks.name: timeframe.isticks
timeframe.isticks.description: Returns true if current resolution is a ticks resolution, false otherwise.

timeframe.isweekly.name: timeframe.isweekly
timeframe.isweekly.description: Returns true if current resolution is a weekly resolution, false otherwise.

timeframe.main_period.name: timeframe.main_period
timeframe.main_period.description: A string representation of the script's main timeframe. If the script is an indicator() that specifies a timeframe value in its declaration statement, this variable holds that value. Otherwise, its value represents the chart's timeframe. Unlike timeframe.period, this variable's value does not change when used in the expression argument of a request.*() function call.

timeframe.period.name: timeframe.period
timeframe.period.description: A string representation of the script's main timeframe or a requested timeframe, depending on how the script uses it. The variable's value represents the timeframe of a requested dataset when used in the expression argument of a request.*() function call. Otherwise, its value represents the script's main timeframe (timeframe.main_period), which equals either the timeframe argument of the indicator() declaration statement or the chart's timeframe.

timenow.name: timenow
timenow.description: Current time in UNIX format. It is the number of milliseconds that have elapsed since 00:00:00 UTC, 1 January 1970.

weekofyear.name: weekofyear
weekofyear.description: The week number of the year, in the exchange time zone, calculated from the bar's opening UNIX timestamp.

year.name: year
year.description: Current bar year in exchange timezone.
